defends: GitHub repository secret-scanning detection config: secret scanning AND push protection are both enabled so a leaked credential is detected and a secret-bearing push is blocked before it lands (defends against credentials-in-files exposure, T1552.001 → T1078; baseline: secure development & monitoring, A.8.15 / A.8.28).

state_document.secret_scanning_enabled: true ⇔ GitHub secret scanning is enabled on the repo (known secret patterns in tracked content are detected and alerted)
state_document.push_protection_enabled: true ⇔ push protection is enabled (a push introducing a recognised secret is blocked at push time, before it reaches the remote)

live_target.note: unknown without live creds; use --state-fixture offline. No GitHub repo security-settings API credentials exist in-container, so a live run cannot fetch the settings document and returns status=unknown (never a silent pass). The SAME validator runs on the fixture and on a (would-be) live document.

self_guard: Before judging any target the EXACT validator runs on fixtures/gh-secret-scanning/{good,bad}/state.json: good (both required keys true) must pass clean; bad (push_protection_enabled=false) is the injected negative control and must be flagged fail. Self-guard not holding ⇒ unknown, never pass.
note: Fixed evidence manifest — the required state-document keys (secret_scanning_enabled, push_protection_enabled) are not model discretion (WORKING_METHOD §1). This is the detection-CONFIG complement of the repo-surface `secret-leak` check, which scans tracked CONTENT directly; this check only asserts the platform detection knobs are on and does NOT duplicate that content-scanning logic. ALL keys must be boolean-true for a pass; any false ⇒ fail; any missing/non-boolean key ⇒ unknown.
